frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: bounded accumulation model fit to perceptual choice behaviour to recover decision bounds

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: decision confidence treated as internal estimate of choice correctness absent external feedback

core_question: Does internally computed decision confidence on one trial adjust the speed-accuracy tradeoff on the subsequent trial?
key_assumptions: agents lack external feedback and rely on internal confidence
perceptual choices are well described by bounded evidence accumulation
decision bound is the adjustable parameter governing speed-accuracy tradeoff
key_findings: fitted decision bounds depended on previous-trial reported confidence
this confidence-driven bound shift was independent of objective accuracy
a centro-parietal EEG component sensitive to confidence predicted the bound increase
interpretations: the brain uses internal confidence signals to continuously update decision policy
low confidence raises the bound, yielding slower but more accurate subsequent decisions
